evals.prompt: I want to serve Llama 3.1 70B for my DSPy app in production. I have 2x A100-80GB GPUs.
evals.expected_output: A vLLM server command with tensor-parallel-size 2, prefix caching enabled, and DSPy connection code using dspy.LM with openai/ prefix and api_base

assertions.name: uses_tensor_parallel_size
assertions.description: Uses vllm serve with --tensor-parallel-size 2 for dual-GPU setup

assertions.name: uses_dspy_lm_openai_prefix
assertions.description: Connects DSPy with dspy.LM using openai/ prefix and api_base pointing to the /v1 endpoint

assertions.name: no_deprecated_hfclientvllm
assertions.description: Does NOT use the deprecated dspy.HFClientVLLM class

assertions.name: includes_api_key_param
assertions.description: Includes api_key parameter set to a non-empty string such as 'none'

evals.prompt: Set up vLLM for running MIPROv2 optimization with a local Llama model on my RTX 4090
evals.expected_output: vLLM server config for a single GPU with high concurrency settings, DSPy optimization code connecting to vLLM

assertions.name: recommends_small_model_for_rtx4090
assertions.description: Recommends an 8B or 13B model that fits in 24GB VRAM rather than a 70B model

assertions.name: includes_max_num_seqs_for_optimizer
assertions.description: Includes --max-num-seqs 256 or similar to handle the optimizer parallel requests

assertions.name: includes_prefix_caching_for_demos
assertions.description: Includes --enable-prefix-caching so shared few-shot demo tokens are cached across optimizer calls

assertions.name: uses_openai_prefix_not_deprecated
assertions.description: Connects DSPy with dspy.LM and openai/ prefix, not the removed HFClientVLLM

assertions.name: shows_miprov2_compile
assertions.description: Shows MIPROv2 compile call using the vLLM-backed LM

evals.prompt: I develop with Ollama on my Mac but want to deploy with vLLM to our GPU server
evals.expected_output: A dual-config pattern using environment variables to switch between Ollama (dev) and vLLM (prod), showing that optimized DSPy programs transfer between backends

assertions.name: no_vllm_for_macos
assertions.description: Does NOT recommend installing or running vLLM on macOS — routes macOS users to Ollama

assertions.name: env_based_switching
assertions.description: Shows environment-based switching between ollama_chat/ provider for dev and openai/ provider for prod

assertions.name: explains_program_transfer
assertions.description: Explains that optimized DSPy programs (instructions and demos) transfer between backends without retraining

assertions.name: vllm_prod_config_with_api_key
assertions.description: Includes vLLM production config with api_base pointing to /v1 and api_key set to a non-empty string

assertions.name: shows_save_load
assertions.description: Mentions program.save and program.load for persisting and loading optimized programs across environments
